explanation.summary: Carbs are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 61 from a base nutrition score of 58. Context lifted it by 3 points.

topPros.title: one of the best budget staple foods
topPros.explanation: It is unusually cheap, filling, and useful in real meals compared with a lot of healthy-food staples.

topCons.title: contains some anti-nutrients
topCons.explanation: Phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat.

topCons.title: can be rough on some people digestively
topCons.explanation: Not everyone handles legumes equally well.

topCons.title: needs some cooking effort
topCons.explanation: A small practical friction versus more convenient foods.

narrationNotes: Context improved the final tier case by 3 points.

closing.summary: Lentils has a genuinely strong overall case, especially one of the best budget staple foods and very filling for the calories. the downsides are there, but contains some anti-nutrients and can be rough on some people digestively do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

scenes.narrationText: fat is 1.1g. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.

scenes.narrationText: carbs is 60.1g. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

scenes.narrationText: protein is 25.8g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

scenes.narrationText: on the plus side: one of the best budget staple foods because it is unusually cheap, filling, and useful in real meals compared with a lot of healthy-food staples. very filling for the calories because the fibre and protein combo gives it strong practical fullness. usually affordable and practical because makes it easy to use as a real staple rather than a niche health food.
